Plot histograms highlighting imputed values (Perseus-style imputation)

plot_perseus_imputation.Rd

If you ran perseus_impute(DAList, save_before_after=TRUE), you can pass: logDat_before = DAList$data_per_contrast[[contrast]]$imputation$before_log2 logDat_after = DAList$data_per_contrast[[contrast]]$imputation$after_log2 or use the global DAList$imputation$before_log2/after_log2 when no per-contrast data exists.

Usage

plot_perseus_imputation(
  logDat_before,
  logDat_after,
  samples = NULL,
  bins = 50,
  facet_ncol = 4,
  overlay = TRUE
)

Arguments

logDat_before

matrix/data.frame of log2 intensities with NAs (pre-imputation)

logDat_after

matrix/data.frame of log2 intensities after perseus_impute()

samples

character/integer vector of columns to plot (default: all)

bins

number of histogram bins

facet_ncol

number of columns in facet wrap

overlay

if TRUE, overlay observed+imputed per sample panel; else stacked

Value

ggplot object (faceted histograms across selected samples)
